docs.title: Account Management
docs.text: This section provides information on logging in to the Thesaurus account and changing an account password.

docs.title: Logging in to the account
docs.text:
Login is required for some activities

Note that there are several actions (e.g., adding and editing contents) that require you to log in first.

  1. Find and select the "Log in" button at the top right corner or click here.

  2. Provide your "Username" and "Password" in dedicated fields and click the "Log in" button.

    Remain logged in

    Check the "Keep me logged in" option to remain logged in after closing the session.

    Password reset

    "Forgot your password?" option does not work due to the lack of an email protocol connection. For a password reset, please contact the Thesaurus administrator.

  3. After completing the procedure, you will be redirected to your user profile page. Moreover, in the top right corner, the user's login appears.

docs.title: Changing the password
docs.text:
1. Log in to the account.

  2. Go to the "Change Password" option on the Main page or click here.

  3. Enter your new password, retype it in the required field and press "Change credentials" button.

  4. Once changed, a confirmation page will be displayed.

docs.title: Appendices
docs.text:
In this section, you can find useful information about:

  • Thesaurus Management System components

  • Division of responsibilities between components

  • Key Terms and Definitions

  • Object feature storage structure

  • Properties in hierarchical dictionaries

  • Inverse properties

  • Important design decisions regarding data modeling

docs.title: Thesaurus Management System components
docs.text:
Dictionary management is provided through four complementary components (click to visit):

  1. Mare Nostrum Thesaurus - a dictionary database that stores individual terms organized into thematic categories in a structured format.

  2. Cradle - a tool for adding new dictionary entries to Mare Nostrum Thesaurus.

  3. Wikibase Query Service (WBQS) - a tool for advanced searching of Mare Nostrum Thesaurus using SPARQL queries.

  4. QuickStatements - a tool for batch editing and bulk importing data into Mare Nostrum Thesaurus.

docs.title: Division of Responsibilities Between Components
docs.text:
The table below presents a summary of the division of responsibilities for the tools described in the previous section.

Tool Responsibility Provided Features Mare Nostrum Thesaurus A dictionary database that stores data in Linked Data format as an advanced network of interconnections. - Browsing data- Displaying hierarchical data- Manually adding new dictionary values Cradle A visual graphical editor supporting the addition of new records to the dictionary database via an intuitive interface with a predefined set of required properties for a given thematic set. - Adding new dictionary values in accordance with the established data model- Adding hierarchically dependent dictionary values Wikibase Query Service An advanced tool for multi-level searching of the dictionary database. Usage requires knowledge of SPARQL syntax as well as the relationships and structures within the Wikidata Mare Nostrum database. - Browsing data- Thematic filtering of database content- Creating data visualizations QuickStatements A tool for batch editing and bulk importing data into the dictionary database. It allows executing sequence-based commands (CSV/V1 formats) to automate large-scale data modifications. - Bulk importing and creating new records- Batch adding, modifying, or removing statements and properties- Automated processing of structured datasets

docs.title: Key Terms and Definitions
docs.text:
Item - a thing, entity or concept. It can be an object, a person, an event, a location, a work of art, or an abstract concept. Items are designated by unique identifiers starting with the letter "Q" followed by a positive integer.

Label - the primary name of an item in a given language. It provides the essential information needed to identify the topic of the item. Labels do not have to be unique.

Description - a short phrase intended to disambiguate items that have identical or similar labels. While descriptions do not need to be unique on their own, two items cannot share both the same label and the same description.

Alias - an alternative name for the same item in a given language. It can be a synonym, colloquial name, abbreviation, or historical name. Aliases improve the searchability of items within the database.

docs.title: Object Feature Storage Structure
docs.text:
Data concerning items (Q) in Mare Nostrum Thesaurus is stored as triplets: item (Q) - property (P) - value, where:

  • Item - indicates the identifier of the entity it refers to.

  • Property - defines a relationship or feature of the given item. Each property has an assigned data type that dictates what values can be entered. The list of available properties in the Mare Nostrum Thesaurus project is available here.

  • Value - represents the actual data associated with the item (it can be another item (Q), a string, a date and time, or geographic point coordinates in the WGS84 system, among others).

Figure below presents a graphical example of the feature storage structure.

(blue rectangle - Item; arrows - Properties; green rectangles - Values)

Image source: Wikidata Query Service Tutorial

docs.title: Properties in Hierarchical Dictionaries
docs.text:
• subclass of (P2) - indicates that one object is a subclass or subset of another. It sould not be confused with instance of (P1).

    Example: A volcano is a subclass of mountain; Mount Everest is an instance of mountain.

  • part of (P20) - indicates that one item is an integral part of another. This property is mainly used in the provenance and chronology dictionaries.

Additional properties used in the dictionaries, but not displayed in the hierarchical structure:

  • modified version of (P18) - indicates that one object is a modified version of another, yet the second object cannot be unambiguously considered identical to the first.

  • said to be the same as (P17) - used when there is a need to add two independent items representing the same object under different names, provided the user does not want to declare the second entity as an alias of the first.

docs.title: Inverse Properties
docs.text:
When manually adding items, it is good practice to add an inverse relationship pointing from the parent item to the child item. Defining this connection is important for efficient navigation between item pages within the hierarchy.

Adding a new Item with Cradle

Cradle is configured to automatically add a specific inverse property of the declared property.

Example: If you are adding the item "beige", which is a subclass of (P2) "hierarchical color of the soil", you should also define the property superclass of (P11) "beige" for the "hierarchical color of the soil" item.

The table below presents the properties used in the Mare Nostrum Thesaurus dictionaries along with their inverses.

Property Inverse Occurrence subclass of (P2) superclass of (P11) all dictionaries excluding "provenance" and "chronology" modified version of (P18) has modification(s) (P19) mainly "sub-category" said to be the same as (P17) said to be the same as (P17) all dictionaries part of (P20) has part(s) (P21) "provenance" and "chronology"

docs.title: Important Design Decisions Regarding Data Modeling
docs.text:
1. Amphora Type, Vessel Form, Sub-category, Morphology, State of Preservation and Surface Treatment Dictionaries

    These dictionaries were modeled exclusively using subclass of (P2), where the parent item represents the dictionary itself and the child item represents a specific dictionary value.

  2. Vessel Part, Harris Matrix Relationships, Trench Parameters, Visual Item Metadata, Linguistic Object Metadata These dictionaries were modeled using subclass of (P2) as hierarchical structures. The parent item represents the dictionary, the first-level child item represents a general "family" of concepts (e.g., "lower part"), and the second-level child item represents a specific type (e.g., "base", "sherd").

    Note

    This approach does not exclude adding a standalone item without a "concept family" at the first child level (e.g., "almost complete").

  3. Provenance Dictionary This dictionary was modeled using part of (P20) on every level of "concept family" (e.g., "Asia Minor" is part of "provenance" and "Phocaea" is part of "Asia Minor").

  4. Chronology Dictionary This dictionary was modeled using part of (P20) similarly to point 3. However, it requires distinguishing between two conceptual meanings:

    • Parts within a single period (e.g., "ER I", "ER II", "ER III" as parts of "ER")
    • Parts of a period spanning two epochs (e.g., "ER" as part of "LH/ER").

    Both contexts use the same property (part of (P20)), though its semantic interpretation differs slightly.

    The figure below provides a graphical exaplanation of the terms used in this section to describe hierarchy nesting levels.

docs.title: Creating a New Record in the Thesaurus - Cradle
docs.text: Cradle is a form-based tool for adding new items to the Mare Nostrum Thesaurus dictionaries. Use it when you need to create a record in a specific dictionary (vessel part, provenance, etc.).

docs.title: Opening Cradle from the Main Page
docs.text:
1. Go to the Thesaurus Main Page.

  2. In the Dictionaries section, select a New [dictionary name] link (for example, New amphora type, New vessel part, etc.).

docs.title: Logging In and Authorizing the Tool
docs.text:
Cradle can only save new items after you log in and grant it permission to edit the Thesaurus.

  1. On the form page, click Log in to save! in the top-right corner.

  2. Sign in with your Mare Nostrum Thesaurus account.

    Account required

    If you are already logged in, you will not see the login page. If you do not have an account yet, follow the steps in Account Management.

  3. On the authorization page, click Allow to permit Cradle to make changes on your behalf.

After a successful authorization, the form for the selected dictionary becomes ready to fill in.

docs.title: Form Layout
docs.text:
Each Cradle form has the same core structure. Required fields are marked with a red asterisk (*).

  1. Labels (required) - the name of the new item in the selected language.
  2. Also known as (optional) - alternative names (aliases) in the selected language.
  3. Descriptions (recommended) - a short description in the selected language that distinguishes the item from similar ones.
  4. Subclass of/Part of (required) - the parent item that places the new record in the dictionary hierarchy.
  5. Additional properties - dictionary-specific fields. Whether they are required depends on the particular dictionary.

Important interface elements:

  • Language selector - a dropdown before each text field in Labels, Also known as, and Descriptions. Choose the appropriate language for the label, description, or alias you are entering.
  • Add (+) and remove (-) buttons - visible after each text field. Click + to add another entry in a different language (pick the language in the dropdown first); click − to remove an extra language entry.
  • Hover over the ⓘ icon next to a property to see its definition.
  • Click the Mare Nostrum logo in the top-right corner to return to the Main Page.
Language

Enter the primary label in English. To add labels, descriptions, or aliases in other languages, use the language selector and the + button next to the relevant field. Available languages: English (EN), Polish (PL), Greek (EL), German (DE), French (FR).

Example form showing described above elements and its dictionary-specific fields

docs.title: Filling In and Submitting the Form
docs.text:
1. Complete all required fields (marked with a red asterisk).
  2. Fill in optional fields as needed for the dictionary you are editing.

  3. When the required fields are complete, the Create new item button appears at the bottom of the form.

    Button not visible?

    If required fields are filled, but Create new item still does not appear, click anywhere outside the currently focused field to refresh the form state.

  4. Click Create new item.

  5. Wait while the page shows Creating item...

  6. When creation succeeds, the Last items created section appears with a link to the new item.

Open the link to review the new record and, if needed, continue editing it on the item page.

docs.title: Creating a Subtype of an Existing Item
docs.text:
By default, the subclass of/part of field points to the main parent of the dictionary. You can instead choose the new item under another existing dictionary entry.

For example: add example paphos as a subtype of paphos, rather than directly under amphora type.

  1. Open the Cradle form for the target dictionary.
  2. In the subclass of/part of field, replace the default parent with the existing item that should be the direct parent.

  3. Complete the remaining required fields and click Create new item.

docs.title: Mare Nostrum Thesaurus - Management
docs.text: This section is dedicated to explain all specific procedures which allow searching, modifying and deleting information from the Mare Nostrum Thesaurus.

docs.title: Available Data
docs.text:
The Mare Nostrum Thesaurus project offers the following data dictionaries (click to explore):

  • amphora type - hierarchical Mediterranean amphora types

  • vessel form - general morphological categories

  • vessel part - preserved component part of a vessel

  • sub-category - detailed product specification (primarily refers to the ceramic fabric/paste, which sometimes implies a specific repertoire of vessel types)

  • provenance - hierarchical dictionary of vessel production sites

  • chronology - hierarchical timeframes

  • morphology - geometric characteristics of fragments

  • state of preservation - state of preservation of a found fragment

  • surface treatment - type of exterior wall finishing

  • Harris matrix relationships - stratigraphic relationships according to the Harris matrix (describing chronological and spatial links between stratigraphic units)

  • trench parameters - parameters defining the physical, material, and methodological characteristics of an archaeological trench. This includes:

    • archaeological remains - types of recovered artifacts and materials

    • context characterization - physical properties of the excavated layer, including its origin, soil color, grain size, and soil density

    • excavation procedures - methods applied during the excavation of the trench

  • visual item metadata - visual object metadata, regarding drawing and image

  • linguistic object metadata - textual and linguistic attributes assigned to an object

docs.title: Searching for a Specific Value
docs.text:
1. If you know the specific record you are looking for, enter it in the dedicated panel located at the top of the interface.

  2. Clicking on an item redirects you to its content, displaying all defined properties.

docs.title: Displaying and Searching Hierarchical Data
docs.text:
Dictionaries structure

Each dictionary is represented by a drop-down list that can be opened by clicking the corresponding link name or by clicking the corresponding dictionary name in the Available Data subsection.

  1. Select a dictionary of interest.

  2. After selecting, an expandable view of the object hierarchy linked to the parent item is displayed (e.g., vessel part).

  3. To display a specific dictionary value, click on the blue "Q" identifier followed by a corresponding number.

docs.title: Manually Adding a New Item
docs.text:
Log in before adding

Adding a new item requires logging in first.

  1. Select the "New item" option from the Main page.

  2. Enter the Label and Description. If aliases exist, they should also be provided here. Complete the process by clicking the "Create" button.

    Language

    Pay close attention to the Language field while adding an item, as an unintentional change may result in incorrect value assignment.

  3. After creating the item, its page will appear on the screen, allowing you to verify the accuracy of the entered data and perform potential edits by clicking the "edit" button.

  4. If the label, description, and aliases are correct, you typically need to define statements pointing to the properties (P) of the item. This is done using the "add statements" button.

  5. To add a new statement, follow steps below:

    • 1 - Enter the appropriate property by its name.

    • 2 - Provide the value representing the given feature.

      Matching types

      Ensure that the added value type matches the property type.

    • 2a (optional) - Add a "reference" indicating the source of the information. Suggested property: described at url (P29).

    • 2b (optional) - Assign a value to the selected property.

      Matching types

      Ensure that the added value type matches the property type.

    • 3 - Save the statement for the item.

  6. After saving the statement, you can assign another value to it ("add value") or create a new statement poining to the item ("add statement").

docs.title: Editing an Existing Item
docs.text:
1. Browse dictionaries for the item of interest (e.g., R'as al Basit).

  2. Click the "edit" button with pencil icon.

    Log in before editing

    Editing an item requires logging in first.

  3. Modify contents and press "save" after completing the change.

  4. Wait for changes to be saved which is indicated by the reappearance of the "edit" button.

docs.title: Deleting an Item
docs.text:
Retrievable Data Loss

This action is not permanent, and the data can be undeleted.

Log in before deleting

Deleting an item requires logging in first.

  1. Browse dictionaries to find the item you want to delete.

  2. (optional) Make sure that the "Tools" pane is displayed on the right side of the selected page. If not, find the "Tools" menu in the upper-right corner of the main content area, expand it, and select the "move to sidebar" option.

  3. In the "Actions" section of the "Tools" pane, select "Delete".

  4. If needed, select a deletion reason from the dropdown menu and provide additional details in the text field (if not, leave the default values). Then, click "Delete page" to remove the item from the Thesaurus.

  5. After deletion, a confirmation message will be displayed.

docs.title: Thesaurus Management Documentation
docs.text: To access the code repository visit Mare Nostrum LAB GitHub (currently not committed).

docs.title: Browsing Thesaurus
docs.text:
1. Go to the Thesaurus main page.

  2. Click on the desired dictionary (e.g., Provenance).

  3. Navigate to the record of interest (e.g., R'as al-Basit).

    Note

    At this point, you can inspect relations between available items.

  4. Inspect a record of interest by clicking on the internal blue identifier of a subject.

  5. After clicking, Thesaurus opens the site associated with the selected record.

docs.title: Editing Contents
docs.text:
1. Browse Thesaurus to find the desired subject.

  2. Click on the "edit" button with pencil icon.

    Login before editing

    Editing contents requires prior logging in. Further instructions here.

  3. Modify contents and click "save" after completing the change.

  4. Wait for changes to be saved which is indicated by the reappearance of the "edit" button.

    Modifiable elements and non-modifiable elements

    You can modify:

    • Descriptive Label
    • Item Description
    • Aliases/alternative names

    • Labels, Descriptions and Aliases in different languages

      (available: English (EN), Polish (PL), Greek (EL), German (DE), French (FR))

    • Properties of an Item:

      • To modify content of existing property use second ""edit" button with pencil icon.
      • To add new property, select "add statements" with plus icon, choose desired property.

    You cannot modify:

    • Item Identifier (Q followed by a number).

docs.title: Adding New Content
docs.text:
1. Selecting the Target Dictionary

    On the "Main page" in the "Dictionaries" section, there are New [dictionary name] links for creating a new item for each dictionary.

  2. Login and Tool Authorization

    The tool requires authorization, which is initiated via the "Log in to save!" button in the top right corner of the form.

    Note

    You will be redirected to the Mare Nostrum Wiki login page, and then to the OAuth page, where authorization is granted by clicking the "Allow" button at the bottom of the message.

    Note

    OAuth authorization requires prior login.

  3. Filling in Data

    • Labels (required) – the name of the item being added in English

    • Also known as – alternative names for the item

    • Descriptions – a short description of the item to distinguish it from others

    • Subclass of/Part of – the parent element for the item being added

  4. Saving

    Once filled out correctly, the "Create new item" button will appear at the bottom of the page. This initiates saving the item to the database.

    During the save process, a "Creating item..." message will be displayed. Upon successful creation, the "Last items created" section will appear at the bottom of the page with a link to the newly added item.

docs.title: Downloading Content
docs.text:
1. Browse Thesaurus to find the desired subjects.

  2. Select the checkboxes next to them.

    Note

    To select all values, check the parent item.

  3. Move to the top of the page and click "Download selected as Arches format".

    Note

    As a result, a wikidata_export.rdf file will be downloaded. You can import it to Arches.
